what_it_does: It is an exhaustive, neutral survey map of the /home/tim/company/runtime/ and /home/tim/company/fabric/ code areas. It catalogs every file's purpose, public API surface, key interconnections, and notable architectural facts, and adds a public-surface index, an interconnect diagram, a discoveries/proofs section, an abbreviation glossary, and a list of unread-but-mapped files.
observations: Covers 77 runtime files plus 4 fabric files (81 total), explicitly excluding 7 channel/session boundary files.

Organizes files into thematic groups such as Core Cognition, Rules/Checks/Marks/Projections, Governance/Coherence, Decision Surface, Corpus/Storage, Authoring, Address/Resolution, Session/Recall, Channel/Board, Context/Routing, Axis/Stack, Rendering/Diagnostics, Routine/Schedule/CI, Anchors, and Fabric.
Includes a Public Surface Index that attempts to enumerate exported functions and classes per file.
Includes a Notable/Surprising Discoveries section that calls out architectural patterns, constraints, proofs, dormancy defaults, embedding strategy, and decoded abbreviations.
Includes a Critical Interconnects section that diagrams how cognition.py, authoring.py, rules.py, the cc_* modules, and fabric/ relate.
States that no code symbols are present in this file (symbols[] = []).
Uses bracketed placeholders like [Listed as mapped; not read in detail] for files that were mapped by header/docstring only.
Heavily emphasizes fail-loud gates, deterministic rule evaluation, file-discovered registries, lineage requirements, and resource reservation.
standouts: An unusually dense, self-declared neutral atlas of 81 files with both per-file tables and system-level architectural commentary.
Strong, repeated emphasis on fail-loud correctness, determinism, and opt-in/dormant-by-default behavior.
The file-discovered registry pattern is treated as a single unifying discipline across roughly 20 registries.
conventions: Markdown front-matter with type, area, and coverage metadata (files_read, files_total, last_read, completeness).
Tabular summaries using File / Purpose / Public Surface / Key Connects / Notes columns.
Neutral, factual tone with explicit avoidance of judgment or ranking.
Use of project-specific abbreviations (G0-G9, C0-C9, RHM) with a decoding glossary.
Citation of internal file paths and approximate line ranges as evidence for claims.
concerns: Some entries are marked as not read in detail, so part of the inventory is inferred rather than verified.
The Public Surface Index is truncated with ... [and many more] instead of being complete.
Dense project jargon and many abbreviations may reduce accessibility for readers outside the project.
The map is long enough that it risks becoming stale relative to the code it describes.
notes: A dense, self-described neutral atlas of the runtime and fabric modules, useful as a lookup table for file purpose, public API, and architectural rationale.
questions: Which exact 7 files were excluded and why are they omitted from this map?
Are the cited line-number ranges anchored to a specific commit, or will they drift as the code changes?
How is this map regenerated or kept synchronized with the codebase?
What tooling validates the coverage metadata (files_read, files_total, completeness)?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a complete neutral inventory of runtime and fabric files, and the file does that while also adding analysis sections (discoveries, interconnects, abbreviations) that remain factual and non-normative.
apparent_themes: File-discovered, declarative registries over hardcoded logic
Reuse of existing primitives rather than parallel implementations
Lineage and provenance as first-class constraints
intention_signals: It tries to provide a single trustworthy reference that lets a reader understand what every runtime and fabric file does, how they connect, and what architectural rules govern them, without prescribing redesign or ranking components.
novelty: It combines a traditional module inventory with an explicit discoveries and proofs layer that calls out architectural invariants (the floor, the reserve, determinism, no-Gemini) as first-class observations rather than burying them in per-file notes.
contribution: It contributes a structured, searchable overview of the runtime+fabric surface area, including per-file purpose/API/connects, a public-surface index, an interconnect diagram, and documented architectural invariants, which a fused system could keep as a living orientation map.
summary_for_embedding: An exhaustive neutral survey map documenting the purpose, public API, connections, and architectural invariants of 81 runtime and fabric files in the company codebase.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as a single factual reference and orientation guide for the runtime and fabric areas, highlighting file-discovered registries, fail-loud gates, and critical interconnections without ranking or redesigning them.
